title: Server-Side Permission Enforcement for Shared Accounts
description:
Extend permission enforcement from client-side UI hiding to server-side validation. Previously, permissions only controlled UI element visibility (e.g., hiding the delete icon if the user lacks delete permission). This task adds server-side checks to all CRUD endpoints so that even if a user bypasses the UI, the server rejects unauthorized operations.

This was implemented incrementally across multiple feature tickets rather than as a single change.
acceptance_criteria: All CRUD operations check server-side permissions before executing
Unauthorized requests return an appropriate error response
Permission checks align with the granted permissions for the user's account context
UI-hidden actions are also blocked at the server level
middleware_changes: Added server-side permission validation to all CRUD controller methods, checking the authenticated user's Laratrust permissions against the current account context before allowing operations.
implementation_notes: Server-side permission checks were added incrementally during other feature development. This ticket served as a tracking item.
comment_summary: Developer noted this was completed across multiple other Jira tasks as an incremental improvement.
